players_state.reasoning:
*Thinking...*

>  I'm starting my turn as Player 2 with a base at (11,3) and 5 credits to work with. My strategy is to focus on economy and scouting—I'll build credit mines at my resource deposits and get a drone out to scout the enemy position. I've identified my credit deposits at (7,4) and (10,6), plus uranium at (11,0) and a central uranium deposit at (6,2), so I'm weighing where to place my first mine for maximum efficiency.
> 
> The main constraint I'm running into is visibility—I'm not sure if my base's detection range covers those deposit locations, so building a mine on a cell I can't see risks the action failing. I'm leaning toward producing a drone first to scout and reveal the map, then building the credit mine once I confirm visibility. With 5 credits, I can afford a drone for 2 credits and a mine for 2 credits, leaving 1 credit leftover. The early economy boost from getting a mine running is worth the risk of committing resources this turn. So the drone spawns adjacent to base around (10,4), which already detects (10,6) at Chebyshev distance 2. I could move the drone toward the center at (8,4) to scout while also covering the mine location at (7,4)—that way I'm building economy and gathering intel simultaneously, though (7,4) is more exposed to enemy raids than the safer corner position.
> 
> Actually, moving to (9,4) is smarter since it's reachable from any possible spawn location within the movement range of 3, and it still detects (7,4) at distance 2. So my plan is: spawn the drone, move it to (9,4) to scout and provide vision for the mine, then build the credit mine at (7,4) to start generating resources.
